items.title: Social Security Trustees: combined trust funds projected depleted in 2034; 83% of benefits payable
items.summary: Social Security’s Trustees project the combined OASI and DI trust funds will be depleted in 2034; at that point, incoming revenue would cover about 83% of scheduled benefits. The OASI (retirement) trust fund alone is projected to be depleted in the fourth quarter of 2032, when about 78% of benefits would be payable without legislative changes.

items.title: HHS proposes shorter LIHEAP household report, removing expired CARES/ARP fields
items.summary: HHS’s Office of Community Services proposed revising LIHEAP’s annual household reporting to reduce burden and remove data elements tied to expired supplemental LIHEAP funding under the CARES Act and American Rescue Plan. The notice estimates total annual burden hours of 1,850,930, with the long format reduced from 67 to 41 hours per response and the short format reduced from 10 to 6 hours per response.

items.title: USDA notes SNAP work-requirement changes from 2025 law; guidance on ABAWD exceptions/waivers pending
items.summary: USDA says it is preparing guidance on how the One Big Beautiful Bill Act of 2025 changes SNAP work requirements for able-bodied adults without dependents (ABAWDs), including changes to exception criteria and waiver criteria. Under current federal rules, ABAWDs ages 18–54 generally face a 3-month time limit in a 3-year period unless they meet an 80-hours-per-month work/work program requirement or qualify for an exemption.

items.title: USDA sets FY 2026 WIC fruit-and-vegetable cash benefit at $26–$52 per month
items.summary: USDA’s WIC policy memo says the FY 2026 inflation adjustment for the Cash-Value Voucher/Benefit (CVV/B) is 11.8% over the FY 2022 baseline. The FY 2026 monthly CVV/B amounts are $48 for pregnant/postpartum participants, $52 for fully or partially breastfeeding participants, and $26 for children.

items.title: USDA publishes WIC income eligibility guidelines for July 2026–June 2027
items.summary: USDA published updated WIC income eligibility guidelines effective July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2027. For 48 contiguous states and D.C., the maximum income guideline for a 1-person household is $29,526 per year ($2,461 per month), and for a 4-person household it is $61,050 per year ($5,088 per month).

items.title: USDA updates income eligibility guidelines for free/reduced school meals and Summer EBT for 2026–27
items.summary: USDA announced the annual Income Eligibility Guidelines used to determine eligibility for free and reduced-price school meals, free milk, and Summer EBT benefits for the period July 1, 2026 through June 30, 2027. USDA says the free and reduced-price thresholds are calculated using the 2026 federal poverty guidelines multiplied by 1.30 (free) and 1.85 (reduced), then rounded up.

items.title: CMS: ACCESS Model enrollment starts July 5 for eligible Original Medicare beneficiaries, with no beneficiary cost-sharing
items.summary: CMS’s MLN Connects newsletter says that starting July 5, eligible Original Medicare beneficiaries can sign up with participating ACCESS Model providers for technology-supported disease management services at low or no additional cost (examples include lifestyle coaching, remote monitoring and medication management). CMS also notes the model includes a co-management payment with no beneficiary cost-sharing, and an ACCESS Directory is expected to launch in July 2026.

items.title: IRS highlights new partial refundability for adoption credit (up to $5,000) after 2024
items.summary: The IRS says that beginning in tax years after Dec. 31, 2024, up to $5,000 (indexed for inflation) of the adoption credit may be refundable, though carried-forward credit amounts can’t be used to calculate the refundable portion. The IRS page also notes that, for tax years beginning after Dec. 31, 2025, limitations on repayment of excess advance premium tax credit are removed.
